summary: The preferred method involves using a lower-molecular-weight-heparin administered through the subcutaneous pathway because it offers reliable efficacy combined with user-friendly application methods suited especially but not exclusively towards immediate postsurgical care scenarios; despite minor variations based upon specific case factors, overall consensus supports its use over other potential modes including intravenous infusion options given logistical benefits associated directly linked back into ensuring optimal prophylactic outcomes without compromising safety standards
